title: You Don't Need React Portals For Modals Anymore (react-call)
summary: react-call is a lightweight utility library that enables calling React components as asynchronous functions. It uses Root and Stack, Upsert Singletons, and Mutation Flow patterns to handle modals and other UI components without React Portals, reducing boilerplate code and state management complexity.
writeup: This Better Stack video covers react-call, a lightweight utility library that lets React components be invoked as asynchronous functions, removing the need for React Portals when building modals. It walks through three core concepts: Root and Stack, Upsert, and Mutation Flow. The patterns address layout isolation, singleton management, and state flow. The takeaway is an alternative architecture for dialog-style components that treats them as awaitable calls rather than Portal-rendered elements.
